reforms.description: Legislation passed at the end of 2010 aims to reform university governance by allowing strategic and management decisions to be made by a management board with outside members, enhancing accountability and efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving university governance is expected to enhance the quality of education and research, thereby contributing to a more skilled workforce and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: Legislation passed at the end of 2010 is designed to reform governance.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in university governance, which is crucial for enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of higher education in Italy.
reforms.source_quote: Legislation passed at the end of 2010 is designed to reform governance along the lines required.

reforms.description: The 2010 reform law creates a state-backed system of loans at competitive rates to support students, aiming to increase university funding and improve access to higher education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to higher education through loans, the reform is expected to increase the number of skilled graduates, thereby enhancing human capital and innovation potential.
reforms.status_evidence: The 2010 reform law creates a state-backed system of loans at competitive rates.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses funding challenges in higher education, potentially increasing student enrollment and completion rates.
reforms.source_quote: The 2010 reform law creates a state-backed system of loans at competitive rates.

reforms.description: The 2010 University law includes provisions to strengthen links between universities and the private sector, aiming to enhance research output and its dissemination.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration between universities and the private sector, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth through improved research commercialization.
reforms.status_evidence: Some provisions of the 2010 University law go some way towards this.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the role of universities in driving innovation through better engagement with the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: The private sector can help to provide both funds and direction to research efforts.

reforms.description: The government plans to facilitate university-business research collaboration by allowing contracts that enable an efficient sharing of costs and benefits between researchers, their institutions, and the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between universities and businesses is expected to improve R&D outcomes and innovation performance, thereby boosting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Further action to facilitate university-business research collaboration is mentioned as a priority.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical gap in Italy's innovation ecosystem by strengthening ties between academia and industry, which is essential for enhancing R&D and innovation performance.
reforms.source_quote: Further action to facilitate university-business research collaboration, especially through allowing contracts that permit an efficient share of costs and benefits between individual researchers, their institutions and the private sector, would help to improve university finances and would boost Italy’s weak showing on many measures of R&D and innovation performance.

reforms.description: The 2011-13 budget introduced a tax credit on investments made by firms commissioning research activities to universities or public research centres, aimed at improving business-academic research links.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages private sector investment in research, enhancing collaboration between businesses and academic institutions, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit was included in the 2011-13 budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards enhancing collaboration between the private sector and research institutions, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The 2011-13 budget introduced a tax credit on investments made in 2011 by firms commissioning research activities to universities or public research centres.

reforms.description: In December 2010, the Italian Parliament approved Law 238/2010, which extends fiscal incentives to a wider range of skilled workers who return to work in Italy, aiming to reverse the brain drain of high-skilled individuals.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing skilled workers to return, the reform aims to enhance the human capital available in Italy, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was approved by Parliament and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant policy change aimed at addressing the critical issue of brain drain, which has long-term implications for Italy's innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In December 2010 the Parliament approved a law (Law 238/2010, 'Incentivi fiscali per il rientro dei lavoratori in Italia') to extend fiscal incentives to a wider range of skilled workers who return to work in Italy.

reforms.description: The new University Reform Act (Law 240/2010) mandates that universities must advertise all academic positions at least at the European level, allowing qualified foreign scholars to apply without prior national accreditation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the recruitment process for foreign scholars, the reform aims to improve the quality of education and research, thereby fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was enacted and universities are now required to follow the new recruitment guidelines.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly alters the recruitment landscape for academic positions, aiming to attract international talent and enhance the overall quality of the higher education system.
reforms.source_quote: The new University Reform Act (Law 240/2010) makes it compulsory for universities to advertise all academic positions at least at the European level.

reforms.description: The 2010 law on University Reform introduced significant changes in university administration, including a new funding allocation model based on teaching and research performance, which aims to enhance accountability and efficiency in the use of public funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By linking funding to performance, the reform incentivizes universities to improve their teaching and research quality, potentially leading to higher economic growth through better education outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was approved by parliament in December 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the governance and funding of universities, aiming to improve their contribution to economic growth through enhanced accountability and performance-based funding.
reforms.source_quote: The 2010 law on University Reform, law 240/2010, approved by parliament in December, provides for changes in three key areas of university administration: governing bodies, recruitment, funding and salaries.

reforms.description: Italy has abolished professor privilege, which previously discouraged patenting activity by allowing professors to retain ownership of intellectual property rights. This reform aims to enhance collaboration between universities and the business sector by providing stronger incentives for researchers to patent inventions and engage in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing patenting incentives, the reform is expected to stimulate innovation and technology transfer from universities to the private sector, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The professor privilege was abolished in 2009.
reforms.importance_rationale: The abolition of professor privilege represents a significant structural change in the intellectual property framework, aimed at fostering innovation and collaboration between academia and industry, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Italy has abolished professor privilege in 2009.

reforms.description: The creation of Technology Transfer Offices (TTO) in Italian universities aims to enhance collaboration between public researchers and private firms, facilitating the transfer of knowledge and technology to the market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: TTOs improve the ability of universities to attract private funding and foster innovation through collaboration with industry.
reforms.status_evidence: TTOs have been established and are operational in various universities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of TTOs represents a significant step towards enhancing university-industry collaboration, although they are still developing and not yet the main channel for such interactions.
reforms.source_quote: One of the most useful initiatives has been the creation of Technology Transfer Offices (TTO), frequently set up on the initiative of individual researchers or groups of researchers.

reforms.description: The FAR (Fondo Agevolazione Ricerca) funding scheme was established to support linkages between public and private research, accounting for a significant portion of public resources devoted to research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing substantial funding for research projects, FAR aims to stimulate industrial applications and enhance collaboration between academia and industry.
reforms.status_evidence: FAR accounted for 46% of overall public resources devoted to research in 2006.
reforms.importance_rationale: FAR represents a significant funding mechanism aimed at enhancing research collaboration, although its impact has been limited by cumbersome procedures.
reforms.source_quote: The main funding scheme is FAR (Fondo Agevolazione Ricerca), which in 2006 accounted for 46% of overall public resources devoted to research.

reforms.description: A tax credit of 40% was introduced for R&D contracts with universities or public research entities, aimed at strengthening links between public research and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit incentivizes private firms to engage in R&D collaborations with universities, thereby fostering innovation and enhancing research outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: A tax credit of 40% was available for R&D involving contracts with universities or public research entities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the tax credit represents a significant policy tool to encourage private sector investment in R&D, although its effectiveness may vary.
reforms.source_quote: In 2007-09, a tax credit of 40% was available for R&D involving contracts with universities or public research entities.
